image.alt: Painted profile portrait of a bearded man in a large white turban and a fur-collared robe

rounds.revealFact: Suleiman the Magnificent ruled the Ottoman Empire at its height for nearly half a century; his subjects knew him not as 'the Magnificent' but as 'the Lawgiver' for his sweeping legal reforms.

image.alt: Classical painting of a fallen commander in a red cloak held up by soldiers amid a dusty ancient battlefield

attribution.fileTitle: File:John Trumbull - The Death of Paulus Aemilius at the Battle of Cannae - 1832.100 - Yale University Art Gallery.jpg
attribution.author: John Trumbull

clues: A commander deliberately let the center of his line sag, luring a far larger army into a pocket that sealed shut around it.
By dusk the trapped force had been annihilated almost to the man, one of the bloodiest single days its army ever suffered.

pairs.a: The Colosseum is completed in Rome
pairs.b: The Step Pyramid of Djoser rises at Saqqara

pairs.a: The Parthenon is completed on the Athenian Acropolis
pairs.b: Hadrian's Wall is built across northern Britain

pairs.a: The Pont du Gard aqueduct is raised in Roman Gaul
pairs.b: The Hanging Gardens are said to rise in Babylon

pairs.a: The Great Library of Alexandria is founded
pairs.b: The Pantheon's great dome is finished in Rome

pairs.a: Rome completes its first great aqueduct, the Aqua Appia
pairs.b: The Grand Canal links China's great rivers

image.alt: Black-and-white photo of a large warship listing and burning beneath towering columns of black smoke

attribution.fileTitle: File:The USS Arizona (BB-39) burning after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor - NARA 195617 - Edit.jpg
attribution.author: U.S. Navy photographer (NARA 195617)

options: The scuttling of the fleet at Scapa Flow

followUpQuestion.prompt: This attack brought which nation directly into the Second World War?

rounds.prompt: Charlemagne is crowned emperor by the pope in Rome on Christmas Day.
rounds.revealFact: Charlemagne's coronation revived the idea of a Roman emperor in western Europe and helped shape the continent's politics for centuries.

rounds.prompt: Where did a Buddhist dynasty raise the world's largest Buddhist temple, a vast stone mandala of stacked, carved terraces?
rounds.revealFact: Borobudur was built around 800 CE on the island of Java and later lay abandoned for centuries beneath volcanic ash and jungle before being rediscovered.
